reasoning: The knowledge cutoff is April 25, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025. This leaves a forecasting horizon of roughly 67 days (about 18% of a year). Historically, Africa and Latin America see an average of ~1–1.5 successful coups per year. During the 2020–2023 intense 'wave' of coups in Africa, the rate approached 2.5 per year, but there have been no successful coups since Gabon in August 2023 (a gap of over 20 months), suggesting a regression to the mean. Extrapolating a base rate of 1 successful coup per year would mean an ~18% chance of one occurring over the next 67 days. Without any identified imminent, specific coup plots in the days immediately prior to the cutoff, I am adjusting slightly below the statistical base rate to 14%.
